title: RAII String Wrapper
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a small class, `DynString`, that **owns** a dynamically allocated `char` buffer. The class acquires the buffer when an object is created and releases it automatically when the object is destroyed (the RAII pattern) -- the code in `main` never frees anything by hand.

## Behavior to implement

- A constructor taking the number of characters; it allocates a `char` buffer of that size.
- A destructor that frees the buffer and prints `Destroyed <N> chars` (where `<N>` is the size).
- `void set(int index, char c)` to store a character at a position.
- `void print() const` that writes every stored character in order with no separators.

Create the object as a local variable in `main` so its destructor fires on scope exit.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 100).
- Line 2: exactly `N` space-separated characters.

## Output

- Line 1: `Stored: ` followed by the `N` characters with no separators.
- Line 2: the destructor's `Destroyed <N> chars` message.

## Constraints

- No `std::string` and no smart pointers -- manage the raw buffer yourself.

title: Reverse Destruction Order
prompt_md:
## Goal

Show, through destructor output, that local objects are destroyed in the **reverse** of their construction order. Build a small RAII class whose constructor and destructor each announce themselves, create three instances in `main`, and let scope exit do the rest.

## Behavior to implement

- A class `IntPool` whose constructor takes a `char` label and an `int` size, allocates an `int` array of that size, and prints `Created pool '<label>' (<size> ints)`.
- A destructor that frees the array and prints `Destroyed pool '<label>' (<size> ints)`.
- In `main`, create three locals with labels `'A'`, `'B'`, `'C'` (in that order) using the three sizes from input. Do not call any destructor yourself.

## Input

- One line with three space-separated integers: the sizes for pools A, B, and C.

## Output

- The three creation lines in A, B, C order, followed by the three destruction lines in reverse (C, B, A).

## Constraints

- No smart pointers and no `std::vector`.

test_cases.label: Sample -- sizes 3 5 2
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Created pool 'A' (3 ints)
Created pool 'B' (5 ints)
Created pool 'C' (2 ints)
Destroyed pool 'C' (2 ints)
Destroyed pool 'B' (5 ints)
Destroyed pool 'A' (3 ints)

test_cases.label: Hidden -- all same size
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Created pool 'A' (10 ints)
Created pool 'B' (10 ints)
Created pool 'C' (10 ints)
Destroyed pool 'C' (10 ints)
Destroyed pool 'B' (10 ints)
Destroyed pool 'A' (10 ints)

test_cases.label: Hidden -- size one each
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Created pool 'A' (1 ints)
Created pool 'B' (1 ints)
Created pool 'C' (1 ints)
Destroyed pool 'C' (1 ints)
Destroyed pool 'B' (1 ints)
Destroyed pool 'A' (1 ints)

test_cases.label: Hidden -- distinct sizes
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Created pool 'A' (7 ints)
Created pool 'B' (2 ints)
Created pool 'C' (9 ints)
Destroyed pool 'C' (9 ints)
Destroyed pool 'B' (2 ints)
Destroyed pool 'A' (7 ints)
